title: phyllanthus pinnatus
intro: Phyllanthus pinnatus, also known as the East Indian cherry or Kamala, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Phyllanthus pinnatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine practices.
